page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-16 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4a server family. It features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, aligning it with memory-intensive workload requirements.

This instance does not include local NVMe SSD storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external network configuration or NAT gateways for public internet access.

The c4a-highmem-16 offers a high resource density of memory relative to compute cores, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that demand substantial memory capacity without requiring a proportional increase in CPU cores. This resource profile is suitable for memory-intensive applications, including medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise applications, and data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-16 is an x86_64 architecture server instance from the c4a family, configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. This configuration establishes a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage for data persistence. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its high memory density relative to compute resources, the c4a-highmem-16 is designed for memory-intensive workloads where memory capacity is the primary constraint. It is well-suited for applications such as in-memory databases, caching systems, and enterprise application hosting.
